reasoning: The federal debt limit was raised in July 2025 via the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act', ending a period of extraordinary measures that had drained the Treasury General Account (TGA) to unusually low levels. Historical precedent and Treasury policy dictate that following a debt ceiling increase, the Treasury rebuilds the TGA to its normal operating range of $750–$850 billion. FRED data confirms this rebuild is already underway, with the TGA rising steadily from $296 billion on July 16 to $370 billion by July 30. Treasury explicitly announced a late-September cash balance target of $850 billion. Because the rebuild requires an orderly issuance of hundreds of billions in T-bills over 1-2 months, the baseline TGA value on August 3 will capture the account mid-ascent (likely in the $400-$450 billion range). Consequently, the August 10 and September 2 values will almost certainly be higher as the TGA continues climbing towards its $850 billion target. For November 2025 and January 2026, the TGA will be circulating within its typical normalized $700-$850 billion range, which is also substantially higher than the August 3 baseline. Given the strong structural logic and Treasury target, probabilities for all dates are very high, trailing downward slightly for distant horizons to account for normal long-term fiscal volatility.
